steps.title: Factor the repeated group
steps.content:
Both terms on the left contain $x+5$, so combine only the coefficients:
$$
(\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{3})(x+5)=-7
$$

steps.title: Simplify the coefficient
steps.content:
Use twelfths to subtract the fractions:
$$
\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{3}=\frac{3}{12}-\frac{4}{12}=-\frac{1}{12}
$$
So the equation becomes
$$
-\frac{1}{12}(x+5)=-7
$$

steps.title: Solve for the variable
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $-12$:
$$
x+5=84
$$
Then subtract $5$:
$$
x=79
$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the two sides of the original equation or enter the equation directly; the intersection gives the same solution. The answer is C.
